prompt:
Create a function 'display_blog_entry' that formats and displays user-submitted blog posts on a webpage. The function should accept two separate inputs for the blog post's title and main content. The function's name should be display_blog_entry. The function should take 2 arguments, i.e., - headline: str, the headline or title of the blog post, - text: str, the full text content of the blog post. The function should return returns a string with the sanitized and HTML-formatted blog post, ensuring safe display on the webpage. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` BLOG_FORMAT = "

{headline}

{text}
" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: BLOG_FORMAT: str, a pre-defined HTML template string for blog posts, containing placeholders for inserting the title and body Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "headline": "My New Adventure", "text": "Today I explored something awesome." }, "output": "

My New Adventure

Today I explored something awesome.
" }
code:
import html BLOG_FORMAT = "

{headline}

{text}
" def display_blog_entry(headline: str, text: str) -> str: """ Formats and displays user-submitted blog posts on a webpage. Args: headline: The headline or title of the blog post. text: The full text content of the blog post. Returns: A string with the sanitized and HTML-formatted blog post, ensuring safe display on the webpage. """ sanitized_headline = html.escape(headline) sanitized_text = html.escape(text) return BLOG_FORMAT.format(headline=sanitized_headline, text=sanitized_text)
